zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Anne Mawd於1538年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾素萊安時，她的父親William Mawde or Hartley26歲，母親Maude Oldfield25歲。她於1556年10月26日與John Hemingway結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1575年7月16日在她的家鄉享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: Mawd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Anne Mawd was born in 1538, in Southowram, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Mawde or Hartley, was 26 and her mother, Maude Oldfield, was 25. She married John Hemingway on 26 October 1556. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 16 July 1575, in her hometown, at the age of 37.

Elle a épousé John Hemingway le 26 octobre 1556. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 juillet 1575, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Anne Mawd(이)가 1538년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 소우토램에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Mawde or Hartley(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maude Oldfield(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1556년 10월 26일 John Hemingway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1575년 7월 16일 그녀의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
